tests.name: denies direct private-object access and verifies authorized round-trip deletion
tests.observable: direct access was denied; authorized bytes matched; deletion removed access

tests.name: accepts an identifier-only message through the live Vercel Queue service
tests.observable: provider returned a message identity for exactly six opaque fields

tests.name: runs the verified fixture end to end and preserves identity after reconstruction
tests.observable: one verified case, succeeded job, private bundle, Queue intent, and execution survived adapter reconstruction

tests.name: uses live Postgres serialization for concurrent starts and one expired-lease recovery
tests.observable: eight concurrent starts collapsed to one identity and one expired lease requeued exactly once

tests.name: reports live durable dependencies ready while the external runner stays unavailable
tests.observable: database, artifact store, and Queue boundary were ready; external runner remained explicitly unavailable

tests.name: exports, restores, and hash-verifies one tenant through live Neon and private Blob
tests.observable: source object removal, fresh-schema restore, manifest re-hash, private bundle read, and sanitized logs all passed

redGreenRecord.evidence: the original provider command exited zero with all six tests skipped when its internal flag was absent
the first restore drill failed because Neon correctly rejected search_path on the pooled endpoint
the command now enables its own live gate and the isolated restore drill uses the provisioned unpooled URL

sanitization: Synthetic bundled fixture only. No credentials, connection strings, provider resource IDs, provider URLs, object keys, test tenant IDs, personal data, customer data, or private repository content are recorded.
